name: Team Hagerty
subject: Standing Up To The Radical Democrats Agenda
body:
#TeamHagerty Updates Straight To Your Inbox
No images? [Click here](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-e-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-q/) February 2, 2020 **Officially Senator Bill Hagerty** [](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-r/) We are so proud to be able to officially say, Senator Bill Hagerty! After being sworn in, Bill said, "I am humbled by the confidence you have placed in me and promise to always represent our Tennessee values proudly. Now it's time to get to work. Thank you for this honor!" **SCOTUS Is At Stake** [](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-y/) Joe Biden and the radical Democrats are working to fundamentally change the Supreme Court of the United States by packing the court with liberal activists. We need strict constitutionalist justices on the Supreme Court to uphold the Constitution and protect our conservative values. Bill will continue fighting to stop the Democrats from packing the court with liberal activists who will legislate from the bench and change the court from what our Founding Fathers intended. **Biden's Actions For More Government** Since taking office, Joe Biden has single-handedly imposed more government into our lives by signing more than 40 Executive Orders. From rejoining the Paris Climate Agreement to stopping the Keystone XL Pipeline's construction to rolling back additional Trump policies, the Biden Administration continues to push radical policies that will cost American jobs and harm our national security. Bill remains committed to standing up for our conservative values, pushing for less government regulation for businesses and protecting our freedoms from the threat of socialism.You can read more about these executive actions on Fox News [**HERE.**](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-j/) **Support Your Local Small Business** Small businesses are the backbone of Tennessee's economy. In order for these local favorites to thrive, we need to do everything we can to support them. That means making every Saturday a #SmallBizSaturday. Join Team Hagerty and shop local this weekend!
## **Press Highlights**
**[Politico:](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-t/) GOP Senator Stocks Office With 13 Former Trump Staffers****[Breitbart:](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-i/) Sen. Bill Hagerty 'Deeply' Concerned With Biden U.N. Ambassador Nominee's Past Praise Of China****[Chattanoogan:](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-d/) Hagerty Hits Biden For "Court Packing" Plan****[Chattanooga Times Free Press:](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-h/) Blackburn, Hagerty Are Sponsors Of Bill Aimed At Ending Taxpayer-Funded Abortion****[Fox 17:](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-k/) Tennessee Senators Blackburn, Hagerty Promise To Fight Back Against 'Socialist Agenda'** | [](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-u/)| [](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-o/)| [](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-b/)| [](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-m/)| [](https://hfs.cmail19.com/t/t-l-mntddt-fuutjhhtj-c/)
---|---|---|---|---
Team Hagerty

Nashville, TN 37215

This is critical, so we'll keep it short, Friend. Just last week, a far-left Democrat launched his campaign to unseat Young Kim. Worse, he raised $200k in 24 hours to do just that. With the House Majority on the line, we have to win this toss-up, top-targeted seat -- _but to defend it from the Left 's cash flood, we have to act fast._

subject: Where do you stand on American energy?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF JOHN BARRASSO
body:
The Keystone Pipeline has not only created thousands of jobs for hard-working Americans, but it has also ensured that the United States would have a reliable source of energy for everyday life. By ending the Keystone Pipeline, Joe Biden proves that Americans everywhere that jobs or reliable energy will not be a priority for his administration.

Friend

The Keystone Pipeline has not only created thousands of jobs for hard-working Americans, but it has also ensured that the United States would have a reliable source of energy for everyday life. By ending the Keystone Pipeline, Joe Biden proves to Americans everywhere that jobs or reliable energy will not be a priority for his administration.

Senator Barrasso needs to hear your thoughts on America's energy independence. Do you stand with American workers who work every day to fight for energy independence? Please, take a moment of your time today to fill out the survey below and make your voice heard.

body:
Pelosi wants to buy your state.
Friend,
**Nancy Pelosi just donated another $100,000 to the National Democratic Redistricting Committee.**
This radical group is dedicated to having Democrats control our redistricting process. If Democrats can take control of our legislative maps, **that means Pelosi can wield power for another then years.**
This is a blatant power grab by Pelosi and it MUST be stopped. She's spending BIG to control our maps, and we can't let her partisan cronies draw the legislative lines in YOUR state.

The Republican State Leadership Committee (RSLC) isn't just another alphabet soup political committee. We're the largest caucus of Republican state leaders in the nation with one unified goal: winning. name: Greg Stanton
subject: This is what leadership looks like
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress
body:
The first two weeks of the Biden administration have proven just how important it is for the United States to have a responsible leader that listens to medical professionals, believes in science, and who sets ambitious vaccination goals for all Americans. It is clear that we must act aggressively and decisively to ensure that we can end this awful pandemic and resume our normal lives. Thankfully, we now have a national strategy that is reducing the spread of this deadly virus.

Last week I shared with you my support for some of the Executive Orders signed by President Biden, [and now I'm encouraged to see how he has increased national vaccination efforts, including increasing vaccines sent to states by 1.4 million doses.](https://act.myngp.com/el/ds9EgKiPya_YdmyM_LpSWGWzwzt-8_u1LWjw0D1TWio=/E3qWDuEvls4CgsVx6At2AszsDRmRjgXHcPv377kjpWc=)

[Can I count on your support to help make sure we can continue to implement President Biden's plan, and bounce back from this pandemic together?](https://act.myngp.com/el/ds9EgKiPya_YdmyM_LpSWGWzwzt-8_u1LWjw0D1TWio=/6vjSIQmEvOewvOanJCvgxyXmyMi7bQyo2nk3JHV96EA=?refcode=X_020221_BidenVax_A)

To rebound from the COVID-19 pandemic it's going to take effort and cooperation from all Americans--including each and every Arizonan. We must continue to do our part. I look forward to working with all of you and leading on this issue. Thank you for your continued support!

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/ds9EgKiPya_YdmyM_LpSWGWzwzt-8_u1LWjw0D1TWio=/6vjSIQmEvOewvOanJCvgxyXmyMi7bQyo2nk3JHV96EA=?refcode=X_020221_BidenVax_A)

Thank you,
\-- _Greg_

Paid for by Stanton for Congress
---
Stanton for Congress
4340 E Indian School Rd.
Suite 21-518
Phoenix AZ 85018 United States

name: Newt Gingrich
subject: I have no choice
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
We came up short and starting this year behind is NOT an option if we are going to win back the Senate. Schumer and the Democrats absolutely CRUSHED us when it came to fundraising this past election cycle, and they have set their sights on doing it again in 2022.
Friend, I am left with no choice except to tell it to you straight.

Schumer and the Democrats absolutely CRUSHED us when it came to fundraising this past election cycle, and they have set their sights on doing it again in 2022.

We need to get a head start in order to beat the Democrats, but we can't do that if we fail to reach our fundraising goals.

name: Tim Scott
subject: Friend, 42…and counting!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TIM SCOTT FOR SENATE
body:
Friend,

Joe Biden has only been in office a few weeks and already he's **_signed a record-shattering number of Executive Actions._ **

**42 ... and counting!**

That's right -- instead of working with Congress, Joe Biden is jamming through his liberal agenda with the stroke of his pen.

Here's just a sampling of these actions:
* Rejoining the job-killing Paris Climate treaty
* Allowing taxpayer funding of abortions overseas
* Canceling the Keystone Pipeline
* Defunding the border wall with Mexico

President Biden's Executive Actions are a laundry list of liberal policies that will hurt hard-working Americans.

We cannot sit on the sidelines anymore and let Joe Biden reverse all our conservative victories from the last four years.

**[Friend, I am counting on grassroots conservatives like you to chip in to help fight back against Biden's liberal agenda.](https://secure.winred.com/tim-scott-for-senate/42-and-counting/?utm_campaign=20210203_TY.100609_tim-scott_t1047196-1260&utm_medium=email&utm_source=TY.100609&utm_content=20210203_first-name-42and-counting/)

[A donation of $10, $25, $50, $100, or whatever you can afford will go a long way in showing Biden and the far left that we will not stand for these liberal policies.](https://secure.winred.com/tim-scott-for-senate/42-and-counting/?utm_campaign=20210203_TY.100609_tim-scott_t1047196-1260&utm_medium=email&utm_source=TY.100609&utm_content=20210203_first-name-42and-counting/)**

I am sure by the time this email reaches you, President Biden will have signed more liberal policies into law -- which is why [**I am counting on you to take action now.**](https://secure.winred.com/tim-scott-for-senate/42-and-counting/?utm_campaign=20210203_TY.100609_tim-scott_t1047196-1260&utm_medium=email&utm_source=TY.100609&utm_content=20210203_first-name-42and-counting/)

Tim Scott

subject: The Poll is now LIVE!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY GARBARINO FOR CONGRESS |

Peter,

_Here’s the situation that small business owners in America are facing right now:_
* Over **100,000** small businesses have closed forever since the beginning of 2020!
* **800** small businesses are shutting down every day!
* Small businesses are still dealing with **curfews** and **capacity restrictions**!

_There’s no denying that these lockdown restrictions have been incredibly damaging to America’s small business community. Yet, Democrat politicians and governors continue to threaten Americans with rolling lockdowns and ever-changing rules._

However, I have a feeling the average American doesn’t agree with strict lockdown policies. **That’s why I’m reaching out to you today. I’ve put together this 🔴 LIVE 🔴 Small Business Poll that ends at MIDNIGHT TONIGHT!_[Peter, do you think it’s time to end these restrictions on small businesses?](http://links.garbarinoforny.com/u/click?_t=7b77702d6650435d80b420c1495e37a8&_m=791311d25d9d4a5b9e14f6461e5619de&_e=Yr_fTiPhR0C58sKuuMrNCRwGPuIBmCuLkrYKAr3hZdnznOkrzBqbGGpE96MFwSQsCCdvMJcktl_58b-Qp7o901XfMpytK1wo8-yATqNfZ_3J6pTOFHOXqMJtLdBRcE1xuUumCoNrIARZsUNMWysYOG16LYZv4KCjM8iFWjI2W-97UpRt0PrzhE9bjNij9QoOt4rwsP8dwnPnGp1-VrAlh88DdKTGorFC6JkX-nTaIo4SBVNaSRssI6Em7ObwJQ2tLelo_yoUFgpVxFixdqUZwsD2Q1OQvwU05ShZtRkoUuaeT6JcBbrtddXZH10w1LhNDm3eJVdIwTt1WDROuuKMA9iCqhdsgYWxj4a3S7qs5d23YUxY5L7jpjsWO15umYJXNKGi-eN_CFo6klTjY50CVz5UhI4YCeS7elylOI7MoxYem0Weon_pR_AHD7Qp-abSsPrzeRJ15B2M1MXwuLJxYMhb480-pxPG8yHe2DtAhuTmNy0IfEglUy_7dV8oVpfV)_**

Peter, our small businesses have gone above and beyond to comply with these restrictions.

They’ve had to change the entire way they do business, they’ve had to invest their own money to stay open, and yet Democrat politicians still won’t be straight with them.

**I need to know who you stand with!_[Make your voice heard, Peter; take this quick 1-Question Live Poll now!](http://links.garbarinoforny.com/u/click?_t=7b77702d6650435d80b420c1495e37a8&_m=791311d25d9d4a5b9e14f6461e5619de&_e=Yr_fTiPhR0C58sKuuMrNCRwGPuIBmCuLkrYKAr3hZdnznOkrzBqbGGpE96MFwSQsCCdvMJcktl_58b-Qp7o901XfMpytK1wo8-yATqNfZ_3J6pTOFHOXqMJtLdBRcE1xuUumCoNrIARZsUNMWysYOG16LYZv4KCjM8iFWjI2W-97UpRt0PrzhE9bjNij9QoOt4rwsP8dwnPnGp1-VrAlh88DdKTGorFC6JkX-nTaIo4SBVNaSRssI6Em7ObwJQ2tLelo_yoUFgpVxFixdqUZwsD2Q1OQvwU05ShZtRkoUubcNDYVvdifa9i1u3eMnBPplUYXGTInBI1MFb8eXLRgmFqovjQZ2woGDAFippFrl0jgaO18EdGHTYC-67YF2T9H_Q0Qyu5ELLyXkmWZ5JFo5KdY1q42ZxIPe4oHwAvQfbDtn0nXrgJkNNZe2LM-Yd3Xbe8QZ4_Q0fQ9-6x8ilORC5m1npNBX-swGTaaBMRANoh5Z9EnV_KpMMi_2BA3Bp2F)_**

name: Rita Hart (personal)
subject: thank you Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
body:
| | | [](https://act.myngp.com/el/MfYA_grQ7Y644d8dT9tcMiRoQzsnoHqv6qzEYyrco-g=/JZJgh1-L9RoDrKFwOR4TncczW-GqAXcq40muk-nZxMQ=?refcode=210203-email) Peter, As we keep moving forward in our fight to count every vote in this race, I wanted to pause for one moment to say **thank you.** When we kicked off this campaign way back in May of 2019, no one could have imagined we would be here now, in a contested election months after Election Day. It's certainly not what I expected, and I'm guessing the same is true for you! It's taking more time, effort, and resources to see this race through to the end, but I'm determined to make sure every voter in this district is heard. I ran for Congress to make sure we listen to all voices, and that's exactly what I'm going to keep doing. **From the bottom of my heart, thank you for being part of this journey through all its ups and downs and twists and turns.** We have a little more work ahead, but in the end, we will know that we did everything we could to make sure _every_ person's vote was counted. All my best, -Rita | Paid for by Rita Hart for Iowa
---
Rita Hart for Iowa
P.O. Box 333
Wheatland IA 52777 United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please [unsubscribe](https://act.myngp.com/el/MfYA_grQ7Y644d8dT9tcMiRoQzsnoHqv6qzEYyrco-g=/lKTKCmNB0f4ATe5E499U1y4_9m5_EKKno-WcxfYVW9o=).

name: Maria Elvira Salazar
subject: [Look] January in Review
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Salazar for Congress |
body:
After recovering from COVID-19, I went straight to work fighting for Florida’s 27th District! I’ve been working on an exciting project... clean_body: After recovering from COVID-19, I went straight to work fighting for Florida’s 27th District! I’ve been working on an exciting project... Looking Back at January 2021! Peter, it’s been quite the month. For the first couple of weeks of January, I battled COVID-19. While I was frustrated I could not be in Washington; I’m eternally grateful for the love and support I received from the community. | | After I had recovered, I was officially sworn into office on January 12th, 2021, and went straight to work fighting for Florida’s 27th District! | The very next day, on January 13th, 2021, I introduced my first bill that was co-sponsored by 8 colleagues. This bill, if signed into law, would hold the Communist Regime in Cuba accountable for its support of terrorist activity across the globe. | My next fight was against Big Tech & Silicon Valley. I called them out for their crackdown of dissenting views on their platforms. I fear this sort of silencing only leads us on a path towards more censorship and Socialism. | Back in Miami, we celebrated the incredible life and legacy of Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. & the 1st Annual Bike Ride for Charity was held to commemorate the day! | While in Washington, I’ve been busy! I was assigned to two committees: Foreign Affairs & Small Business! I’m honored to be able to fight directly for struggling small business owners in South Florida! | I also called out the horrible mistreatment of our brave men & women of the National Guard! Offering them a warm place to stay and, of course, Cuban Coffee! | Lastly, I’ve been working on an exciting project with some of my fellow freshman Republicans. 🇺🇸 The Freedom Force 🇺🇸 Our Mission? Defeating Socialism in America! | Peter, I know January was a tough month for many Americans, and that’s why it's so important to shine a light on the hope & good in this world. Together we will face the challenges ahead, and I’m proud to be fighting for you in Congress! Thank you, Maria Elvira Salazar P.S. Want to support our campaign even further? | | | Paid for by Salazar for Congress | | P.O. Box 431332, Miami, FL 33243 | | This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com | | |

subject: The Democrats are after our guns
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Texans for Ronny Jackson
body:
I know their radical plan.
[](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pwqNsbqop0p5-0KLkEkF6BPZObDL73effS9PZI6zzmrs3lSnmEz6u3Ym8WbdGNN1p4F0ynjG6gQlCZwNR37zac5fO3dyvu8gU_0xs21Qza59B0TujkMCu0SOHNKm9fDeyhAKJo8RJB28Gv6Xst0iPAPMoqXSCaVIpkar3eheHDREYkaDvUylYkzqwVdQfZO-xVAjNd9dTfIgnqt8YkXBOsfBG64oqzfqm7dFkhBmgDlKcXrXz8jdk_Wcu1eKS84FUs5naUbCulxUR19X7YX8hdxKSmC3ZZntD41A_cekZ4RUeM0bcyrXLcn-z4KXUhygszjdT9LOTA9b2MbgPpjFeciIQx06h12pQnRBJxlXAYrmOftU-7Dob886MZyr-9wje-upX88l80Gaw3jTloyVjkZULc1R7Nc3AcSk2BvmxbOvFN8zjx6a0qzoh3DGA7LBIZrRxVXxOjld-qnCjjbviK8Fc6SQGWda&c=HeWn6T7pYN0yLOP9EkVN1X0ZWEOB2-T-6giOwSkGonBwKHndOOjBTw==&ch=nk3dZHJMeDCmFZrV51ZjOXlqHZEaOVaXsTOKy3WUTxd-atZcBVRoGA==)
Friend, This is Congressman Ronny Jackson, and I represent the most AMERICA-FIRST Congressional district in the entire nation. **Just this last week, liberal Congresswoman Sheila Jackson Lee introduced a bill to Congress that would FORCE every gun owner in America to register their firearms in a NATIONAL database that all of their Radical Left allies could access.** The bill would also CRIMINALIZE any possessions of magazines over 10 rounds. **We CANNOT let them get away with this, Friend.** Here in Texas, we VALUE our right to bear arms. Our ability to own and use our firearms keeps us safe from criminals who would do us SERIOUS harm. **That 's why I'm working day and night to STOP any encroachment on your 2nd Amendment Rights. ** [**Do you think you could contribute TODAY and help support me in my efforts to STOP gun-grabbing Democrats?**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pwqNsbqop0p5-0KLkEkF6BPZObDL73effS9PZI6zzmrs3lSnmEz6u3Ym8WbdGNN1p4F0ynjG6gQlCZwNR37zac5fO3dyvu8gU_0xs21Qza59B0TujkMCu0SOHNKm9fDeyhAKJo8RJB28Gv6Xst0iPAPMoqXSCaVIpkar3eheHDREYkaDvUylYkzqwVdQfZO-xVAjNd9dTfIgnqt8YkXBOsfBG64oqzfqm7dFkhBmgDlKcXrXz8jdk_Wcu1eKS84FUs5naUbCulxUR19X7YX8hdxKSmC3ZZntD41A_cekZ4RUeM0bcyrXLcn-z4KXUhygszjdT9LOTA9b2MbgPpjFeciIQx06h12pQnRBJxlXAYrmOftU-7Dob886MZyr-9wje-upX88l80Gaw3jTloyVjkZULc1R7Nc3AcSk2BvmxbOvFN8zjx6a0qzoh3DGA7LBIZrRxVXxOjld-qnCjjbviK8Fc6SQGWda&c=HeWn6T7pYN0yLOP9EkVN1X0ZWEOB2-T-6giOwSkGonBwKHndOOjBTw==&ch=nk3dZHJMeDCmFZrV51ZjOXlqHZEaOVaXsTOKy3WUTxd-atZcBVRoGA==) [**DONATE NOW**](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pwqNsbqop0p5-0KLkEkF6BPZObDL73effS9PZI6zzmrs3lSnmEz6u3Ym8WbdGNN1p4F0ynjG6gQlCZwNR37zac5fO3dyvu8gU_0xs21Qza59B0TujkMCu0SOHNKm9fDeyhAKJo8RJB28Gv6Xst0iPAPMoqXSCaVIpkar3eheHDREYkaDvUylYkzqwVdQfZO-xVAjNd9dTfIgnqt8YkXBOsfBG64oqzfqm7dFkhBmgDlKcXrXz8jdk_Wcu1eKS84FUs5naUbCulxUR19X7YX8hdxKSmC3ZZntD41A_cekZ4RUeM0bcyrXLcn-z4KXUhygszjdT9LOTA9b2MbgPpjFeciIQx06h12pQnRBJxlXAYrmOftU-7Dob886MZyr-9wje-upX88l80Gaw3jTloyVjkZULc1R7Nc3AcSk2BvmxbOvFN8zjx6a0qzoh3DGA7LBIZrRxVXxOjld-qnCjjbviK8Fc6SQGWda&c=HeWn6T7pYN0yLOP9EkVN1X0ZWEOB2-T-6giOwSkGonBwKHndOOjBTw==&ch=nk3dZHJMeDCmFZrV51ZjOXlqHZEaOVaXsTOKy3WUTxd-atZcBVRoGA==) Every time the Democrats get in power they attempt to destroy our way of life, but as long as I'm in Congress, I won't let it happen. Stand with me and let's show the Democrats that our 2nd Amendment rights aren't up for debate. [**Chip-in HERE and help me protect our gun rights >>> **](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pwqNsbqop0p5-0KLkEkF6BPZObDL73effS9PZI6zzmrs3lSnmEz6u3Ym8WbdGNN1p4F0ynjG6gQlCZwNR37zac5fO3dyvu8gU_0xs21Qza59B0TujkMCu0SOHNKm9fDeyhAKJo8RJB28Gv6Xst0iPAPMoqXSCaVIpkar3eheHDREYkaDvUylYkzqwVdQfZO-xVAjNd9dTfIgnqt8YkXBOsfBG64oqzfqm7dFkhBmgDlKcXrXz8jdk_Wcu1eKS84FUs5naUbCulxUR19X7YX8hdxKSmC3ZZntD41A_cekZ4RUeM0bcyrXLcn-z4KXUhygszjdT9LOTA9b2MbgPpjFeciIQx06h12pQnRBJxlXAYrmOftU-7Dob886MZyr-9wje-upX88l80Gaw3jTloyVjkZULc1R7Nc3AcSk2BvmxbOvFN8zjx6a0qzoh3DGA7LBIZrRxVXxOjld-qnCjjbviK8Fc6SQGWda&c=HeWn6T7pYN0yLOP9EkVN1X0ZWEOB2-T-6giOwSkGonBwKHndOOjBTw==&ch=nk3dZHJMeDCmFZrV51ZjOXlqHZEaOVaXsTOKy3WUTxd-atZcBVRoGA==) Thank you, Ronny Jackson

name: Leslie Rutledge
subject: President Obama's 3rd term
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Rutledge for Governor

Joe Biden just undid four years of strong conservative policies that put American workers FIRST .

name: Judicial Watch Corruption Chronicles
subject: Senator on Panel that Oversees IRS asks Tax Agency to Strip Conservative Group of Nonprofit Status Over COVID-19 “Superspreader” Events
body:
Reminiscent of the Obama Internal Revenue Service’s (IRS) witch hunt of conservative groups, a U.S. Senator who sits on the committee that oversees the tax agency is pushing it to revoke a student charity’s nonprofit status.

---
| | | Because no one is above the law! | February 03, 2021
---|---|---
| | [](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52784&L=21939&F=H&)
---
# [Senator on Panel that Oversees IRS asks Tax Agency to Strip Conservative Group of Nonprofit Status Over COVID-19 “Superspreader” Events](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52784&L=21939&F=H&)
Reminiscent of the Obama Internal Revenue Service’s (IRS) witch hunt of conservative groups, a U.S. Senator who sits on the committee that oversees the tax agency is pushing it to revoke a student charity’s nonprofit status. The veteran lawmaker, Rhode Island Democrat Sheldon Whitehouse, says the conservative student organization, Turning Point USA, should lose its nonprofit rating because it held large events that could help spread COVID-19. In a letter to IRS Commissioner Charles P. Rettig the senator describes the gatherings as “superspreader” events. He specifically mentions a Palm Beach, Florida winter gala at the Mar-a-Lago Club famously owned by former President Donald Trump.
[Read More](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52784&L=21939&F=H&)
|
---
| |

### [Judicial Watch and Daily Caller News Foundation Appeal Court Decision about Hidden Biden Senatorial Records ](https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/wta/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=52784&L=22007&F=H&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvcHJlc3MtcmVsZWFzZXMvanctZGMtYmlkZW4tcmVjb3Jkcy8/dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1kZXBsb3llciZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPWVtYWlsJnV0bV9jYW1wYWlnbj1jb3JydXB0aW9uK2Nocm9uaWNsZXMmdXRtX3Rlcm09bWVtYmVycyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD0yMDIxMDIwMzE2MjMwNQ==) Judicial Watch Judicial Watch announced it filed a notice of appeal to the Supreme Court of Delaware, asking for a reversal of the opinion of the Superior Court of Delaware, which is blocking a state Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for access to records about President Joe Biden’s senatorial records held by the University of Delaware. Biden’s papers include more than 1,850 boxes of archival records from his senate career. clean_body: Reminiscent of the Obama Internal Revenue Service’s (IRS) witch hunt of conservative groups, a U.S. Senator who sits on the committee that oversees the tax agency is pushing it to revoke a student charity’s nonprofit status. | | | Because no one is above the law! | February 03, 2021 ---|---|--- | | = = Reminiscent of the Obama Internal Revenue Service’s (IRS) witch hunt of conservative groups, a U.S. Senator who sits on the committee that oversees the tax agency is pushing it to revoke a student charity’s nonprofit status. The veteran lawmaker, Rhode Island Democrat Sheldon Whitehouse, says the conservative student organization, Turning Point USA, should lose its nonprofit rating because it held large events that could help spread COVID-19. In a letter to IRS Commissioner Charles P. Rettig the senator describes the gatherings as “superspreader” events. He specifically mentions a Palm Beach, Florida winter gala at the Mar-a-Lago Club famously owned by former President Donald Trump. = | | | = The Daily Caller “We have no idea why the University of Delaware is hiding these materials that the public should have every right to see and we are grateful for Judicial Watch and our local counsel for pushing this case hard,” DCNF President Neil Patel said. “When people ask why our institutions are no longer trusted, they should look up this case.” = == Judicial Watch Judicial Watch announced it filed a notice of appeal to the Supreme Court of Delaware, asking for a reversal of the opinion of the Superior Court of Delaware, which is blocking a state Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request for access to records about President Joe Biden’s senatorial records held by the University of Delaware. Biden’s papers include more than 1,850 boxes of archival records from his senate career. == Judicial Watch I first met Russell Welch at a cheap motel on the outskirts of Mena, Arkansas, in the summer of 1994. It was a searing hot day and I had left the door open for some air and I heard a car slowly drive across the gravel and stop. I looked up and Russell’s gaunt shadow crossed the doorway. | CLIPS | | | | | | Keep our investigators on the job uncovering the truth: | = | | | | ---|---|--- = = = Judicial Watch, Inc. 425 3rd St Sw Ste 800, Washington, DC 20024 202.646.5172 (C)2017-2021, All Rights Reserved = |

name: Ro Khanna
subject: Hedge funds must play by the same rules
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ro for Congress
body:
Hedge funds and stock market insiders used their power to control the market and keep Main Street out of Wall Street.
#
There is fundamental inequality present in our stock market.
Just last week, when Americans tried to invest in the stock market, Wall Street hedge funds and trading platforms such as Robinhood prevented buyers from purchasing shares of Gamestop, AMC, and 11 other companies.
**Hedge funds and stock market insiders used their power to control the market and keep Main Street out of Wall Street.** They used these self-imposed restrictions to create a stock market that works for themselves, not the people.
But together, we can demand market equality. **[Will you join me in calling for an investigation into Robinhood, regulations for short selling, and rules against hedge funds using their power to create a rigged game? Sign our petition today >>](https://act.rokhanna.com/go/5186?t=1&akid=1621%2E946728%2Eg8Cy1y)**
**[Sign your name >>](https://act.rokhanna.com/go/5186?t=2&akid=1621%2E946728%2Eg8Cy1y)**
Hedge funds must play by the same rules that everyone else follows — but to do that we need to reform Wall Street and hold them accountable for this market manipulation.
The CEO of Robinhood is expected to testify before the U.S. House Financial Services Committee in two weeks, but market inequity goes beyond this one instance. We need more hearings that focus on hedge funds and clearing houses’ ability to control the stock market and make billions of dollars doing so, _all while over 50% of Americans are not involved in the stock market._
It’s time to fight for a stock market that works for all. **[Sign onto our petition demanding market equality through real reform and regulations.](https://act.rokhanna.com/go/5186?t=3&akid=1621%2E946728%2Eg8Cy1y)**
Let's do this,
Ro Khanna

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ro-emails?refcode=em20210203-1621-footer&t=4&refcode2=1621_946728_g8Cy1y&akid=1621%2E946728%2Eg8Cy1y)** | [](https://www.facebook.com/RoKhannaUSA) | [](https://twitter.com/RoKhanna)
---|---|---
Paid for by Ro for Congress
PO Box 3513, Santa Clara, CA 95051
Email us: [info@rokhanna.com](mailto:info@rokhanna.com)

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we stay in touch with you, but if you need to remove yourself from our email list, [click here to unsubscribe](https://act.rokhanna.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=5&akid=1621%2E946728%2Eg8Cy1y).

body:
Friend,

The political games being played by the radical Left are not only pathetic, but harmful to Americans. A Democratic Senator is going to preside over the impeachment trial of a private citizen because the Chief Justice of the Supreme Court won't get near their hyper-partisan circus.

_**Democrats are prioritizing their vindictive political agenda over the needs of the American people.**_

Republicans are ready to work with Democrats -- just like we did last year -- to provide relief for struggling Americans during the largest health crisis of our lifetime. Instead, Chuck Schumer and Nancy Pelosi are wasting time trying to remove a President who is no longer in office. It's absurd and it needs to end NOW.

_**_[Stop Pelosi and Schumer's Circus >>> Add Your Name](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001AYWW5ZWJwMWVfoHiJ270QGR2V5rw5S8UvFJiulaUkyyhebUnpVAqumDQne8oKqY49uPav8aWajIs6fjQ65JtvNzub2tpK7GOegQ7JE3Fbjs66prlG3RESqXqd7C-03X47CXh1m40-LU-MvjmI5AF4Vly9Frt5caFVwGiiHbxpyZ06FKihX1v8FjbRgc2zslEAt__kgBPw_xGBwyeaEiF6mmIvt75nJ631LSW5xP-TltTIWV1ms_zdw2gLxYFO_Hxfb03JmL2D36_Zv0gHbIkcWY7BdFYU33uFGIo80zDamSGgmtjRC7TDfwgQ74uyZOlXffw6XJDcZlXJdqhUlXxD-nOsXfrDr3KbG7Zn8Ik-JyGg6bHNZ5r7Tb6vLg_7rPvQz_nnFv2xk4yOWGe5wn-k1WUDHQlOAGeG4NxtUyvEL-Xar5fQOrnDQ==&c=QmJ-mMM0zFifWD05PoZ0WGnCYbq9yBRKWqr3hKJakLBUbgfDhEbBPg==&ch=d4UcO1fkZ6sq3-OYakQfblqmNularjEblDmBELGkguVRI7AFlqrLMQ== "Add Your Name")_**_

This impeachment process helps no one.** Democratic Leadership should be focused on working with Republicans to provide relief for struggling American families, not engaging in political showmanship. __**[Stop Pelosi and Schumer's Circus >>> Add Your Name](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001AYWW5ZWJwMWVfoHiJ270QGR2V5rw5S8UvFJiulaUkyyhebUnpVAqumDQne8oKqY49uPav8aWajIs6fjQ65JtvNzub2tpK7GOegQ7JE3Fbjs66prlG3RESqXqd7C-03X47CXh1m40-LU-MvjmI5AF4Vly9Frt5caFVwGiiHbxpyZ06FKihX1v8FjbRgc2zslEAt__kgBPw_xGBwyeaEiF6mmIvt75nJ631LSW5xP-TltTIWV1ms_zdw2gLxYFO_Hxfb03JmL2D36_Zv0gHbIkcWY7BdFYU33uFGIo80zDamSGgmtjRC7TDfwgQ74uyZOlXffw6XJDcZlXJdqhUlXxD-nOsXfrDr3KbG7Zn8Ik-JyGg6bHNZ5r7Tb6vLg_7rPvQz_nnFv2xk4yOWGe5wn-k1WUDHQlOAGeG4NxtUyvEL-Xar5fQOrnDQ==&c=QmJ-mMM0zFifWD05PoZ0WGnCYbq9yBRKWqr3hKJakLBUbgfDhEbBPg==&ch=d4UcO1fkZ6sq3-OYakQfblqmNularjEblDmBELGkguVRI7AFlqrLMQ== "Stop Pelosi and Schumer")**__

Republicans are ready to work with Democrats -- just like we did last year -- to provide relief for struggling Americans during the largest health crisis of our lifetime. It's absurd and it needs to end NOW. == "Add Your Name" This impeachment process helps no one. We need to hold the Democrats accountable for choosing a divisive political show over the needs of Americans. == "Stop Pelosi and Schumer" Join us and tell Schumer, Pelosi and the rest of the Democrats that Americans want leadership focused on the future of our country, not a political circus conjured up for their own vengeance. name: =?utf-8?Q?Defend=20America?=
subject: Biden is bypassing Congress...
body:
President Biden has signed 45 executive actions during his first two weeks, and will likely sign many more. | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us10.campaign-archive.com/?u=7c58654388ec83b4612750ef7&id=077b483b67&e=24057c0240)
---
| |
---
| | Dear Peter, Today marks the beginning of President Biden's third week in office. He's signed a total of 45 Executive Orders, dealing with issues like open-border immigration, taxpayer-funding of abortion, and the radical Green New Deal. It is hard to comprehend the breadth and scope of policies that President Biden is advancing by bypassing the legislative process and using the swift stroke of a pen. _**More importantly, we must ask that if President Biden has already called for this many radical, far-left policies in just two weeks...**_ _**What will America look like in six months, or one year?**_ [Politico recently quoted one of Joe Biden's strongest allies in Congress as having said:](https://patrickmorrisey.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c58654388ec83b4612750ef7&id=6aa4f9bf5c&e=24057c0240)
---
| | "It will save our [Democrat] majority if he takes that approach with everything he does... Don't try to go around them. But, if they refuse to do it, use his executive powers and do it. And let them take you to Court."

\- House Majority Whip Jim Clyburn (D - SC)
---
| This country is not a dictatorship. And the President is not a king. The President does not get to demand legislative action from Congress, and then sign an executive order if Congress doesn't comply. _**This is why the Defend America movement has received incredible amounts of support from individuals all across the nation. The People understand that if someone does not STAND AND ACT NOW to block President Biden's Federal overreach, then our conservative values are lost. **_ Attorney General Patrick Morrisey, with his years of experience fighting Federal overreach, will not rest until the Rule of Law is defended and your families and jobs are protected. Would you be willing to chip in $5 today to ensure that the Biden Administration's unlawful and unconstitutional actions are stopped?

---
| [Chip in $5 to Stop Joe Biden](https://patrickmorrisey.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7c58654388ec83b4612750ef7&id=3e2cbc4d55&e=24057c0240 "Chip in $5 to Stop Joe Biden")
---
|
Keep the faith,

Team Morrisey

subject: can we text you?

body:
It's an honor to have the continued support of activists like you as Sarah fights back against the radical left's socialist nightmare. [](http://links.shsforgov.com/u/click?_t=1a739425fcea4563aabd0234355111c0&_m=2c1045516fd9454dac1dead5967334ba&_e=ZXlgK6PIw3CmLkOOywb9DoqAmOnkkeZhpi8eTOPTBEtTf8W8DzfK4pZiWBPJrgevIPMhhsKUDuoFDvLMP2CDjj-UK14thxAu8wo6dv8K977G5PPFP32RbYHPTcZuiHAjUiskhIvKZDBqDYuJFjH88KIyiFBHG1GuiIp2hK1T0AcC-DdKVYIgnZBOlsHEchlLumVkeRDyNTYAiffggu8iRVGvkTYcF_Y0mN_lpFfGwHUOQSvNlbRQfpNqYoixB7-QDkR2BtTmHGacPzrqv1crv1VlwEQGBbh3o_g2LDtZuXzEzmSwwQO6GU77-rHIDm2hjamQ-nvXo4bx72VvO7W99WYNC4joSusfHWWyolXZmqQjYYpouzLsXWUPepm_2Kp08sf7Teki2PqME0-uOKmiQYlVpWjc9sWjhhKskHtwXYdNXOgOD7GQCQMwxM-_nt2b-BrIyarLzECmos8dBJrdJQ%3D%3D) | |
---
Peter,

If we haven't told you in a while - we'll say it again: it's grassroots supporters like you who keep this campaign going strong every single day.

It's an honor to have the continued support of activists like you as Sarah fights back against the radical left's socialist nightmare.

And as we move forward, the stakes are only going to get higher, Peter.

That's why it's vital that we keep in touch! We want to make sure we can reach you via text message when necessary.

Arkansas law limits contributions to $5,600 per election cycle. Each individual, political party committee, legislative caucus committee or approved political action committee may contribute up to $2,800 for the primary election and $2,800 for the general election -- for a total of $5,600.
Corporate contributions are not permissible.
Contributions are not tax deductible for Federal income tax purposes. Individuals may take a tax credit on Arkansas tax returns, up to a total of $50 per individual for political contributions made each year.
Contributions from foreign nationals are prohibited by law.
State law requires that we collect and report name, address, employer and occupation for each contributor.

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: The State of our State
body:
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
Earlier tonight, I delivered the 2021 State of the State address and discussed all we have accomplished together these past six years to build a stronger, more equitable and more resilient Rhode Island. You can watch the video below, or keep reading to see the full written remarks.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pnDwRdydvvzYX0ktP76nUiLJzK6sfC6_XiKN2nSj8asKl-kv1yJYxShpeOR78ap7sp_UK72BeO8Z6rMUpBTf9fztm1aFqnp92u_FPr52MLUAoStWJcH76EPPV-mr94TnclCF0DWJMvTqBIqPQjpgDBJPH2E54xROoQkDaOqPsCDB0ClFO7UNItiSepi_Vz9H1ni8_CwzAMffbnru2rq5Tg==&c=ReOGMOB4mIuxuPAxFc3C9OnNJordRI_UW6TmHtoJ86MTEm3r0be-dg==&ch=VGUdzBWYATIyj20o7jLpUgDQN5NJh6VAucut5Cc1SUyRO9iXqRmDXw==)
---
| Speaker Shekarchi, Senate President Ruggerio, my fellow Rhode Islanders: We come together tonight, united by the love we share for our great state and our belief that our best days are ahead. Each year, we gather in this chamber with the General Assembly, General Officers, judges, cabinet members, local leaders, and so many others. I am missing these colleagues tonight as we have to adapt our traditions because of COVID-19 - just as we have all done so many times this year.
2020 was a year we will never forget. Together, we faced challenges and tragedy like never before. But we got through it. We did what Rhode Islanders have always done: we innovated, and we persevered. And tonight, we allow ourselves to envision a brighter, more joyful, more prosperous time ahead. A time when we can be together again with family and friends - when we can work, learn and enjoy each other's company without so many restrictions.
2021 will be our year of rebuilding.
There's a lot of work to do, but I stand here confident that we have laid the foundation for a stronger and more equitable Rhode Island. And I know that Lt. Governor McKee is prepared to lead our state. He's passionate, he's experienced, he's committed to public service, and he's going to do a great job.
I want you to know that there will be no disruption to our state's COVID-19 response, and Lt. Governor McKee has committed to maintaining the entire statewide response team. I also want you to be reassured, as I am, that we are in a good, stable place. Our weekly percent positive is 3.3% - the lowest it has been in over three months. Our hospitalizations continue to decline. We have performed over 2.5 million tests and administered over 100,000 vaccines. By every measure, we're on the right path, and the end is in sight.
Tonight, as we reflect on the past year, let's begin by recognizing the nearly 2,200 families across our state that have lost a loved one to COVID-19. This virus robbed so many of you of the chance to properly say goodbye. To every Rhode Islander who has lost someone: we grieve with you and we pray for you. Tonight, we're lighting the dome of the State House in memory of your loved ones.
2020 was a year of heartbreak and struggle. Every single day I heard from healthcare professionals working overnight shifts without a day off; parents balancing work with virtual learning; waiters, waitresses, cashiers and clerks out-of-work, surviving on unemployment insurance, worried about when, or whether, their jobs would come back; small business owners who wanted to stay open and make payroll but didn't know how much longer they could hold on; Rhode Islanders struggling with addiction; and families devastated by overdose.
And we also know that women have borne the brunt of this economic crisis. Women have lost jobs in record numbers. Women are disproportionately on the frontlines as teachers, nurses, childcare workers, and caregivers. And moms have carried the load at home during the pandemic.
But at the same time, our challenges have united and inspired us. Healthcare workers and teachers came out of retirement to join the response. We built field hospitals in a matter of weeks. We established best-in-the-nation COVID-19 testing; provided housing and childcare to frontline workers; successfully set up distance learning; and got kids safely back to the classroom. And now, our mission is to vaccinate every Rhode Islander as quickly as possible.
None of this has been easy, and everyone has sacrificed. To every Rhode Islander who has missed out on celebrating holidays and milestones, who has canceled travel, who has worn a mask every day - thank you. You have saved lives.
While much has been taken from us, the strength and spirit of who we are as Rhode Islanders endures.
When I think about the past year, I think about neighbors checking in on one another, offering to pick up groceries and prescriptions, writing letters and connecting virtually. I think about businesses that changed their operations overnight to produce masks, gowns, and hand sanitizer. I think about all the state employees, and the men and women of the Rhode Island National Guard, who sacrificed so much to serve on the frontlines. You inspire me and give me hope. It's because of you - because of your relentless community spirit - that I stand before you tonight more confident than ever that the state of our state is strong because the people of our state are strong.
Rhode Island is prepared to meet this moment. I know that because of the work we have done together over the past six years to make our state stronger. We have made investments in Rhode Islanders - in skills and education, job creation, infrastructure, healthcare, equity and sustainability. On this foundation, we will build back better. And we'll make sure that no one is left behind. It will take all of us to rebuild this economy, and that means everyone must reap the rewards.
Six years ago, on a frigid, snowy day, I stood on the State House steps and vowed that, together, we would ignite Rhode Island's comeback.
At that time, we were stuck in the wake of the Great Recession. Many believed our problems were too big to solve. Our system was too broken. As a result, we had lowered our expectations and allowed more than a bit of cynicism to creep in. But on that day, we committed to a fresh start.
Rhode Islanders stepped up and became part of the solution - committing to do things differently, to believe that change is possible, and to harness that same eternal optimism of our founders as we charted a new course.
We rolled up our sleeves and we got to work.
When we started, we didn't have any of the job creation tools we needed, so we designed them from scratch. And I want to especially thank my colleagues in the legislature for your partnership in those early weeks of my administration and every day since. Together, we went from having one of the highest unemployment rates in the nation to having more jobs than ever before.
We worked with businesses to give Rhode Islanders the skills they need to get actual jobs in growing fields. And when COVID-19 hit, we supercharged this model to help 7,000 Rhode Islanders who had been laid off during the pandemic get back to work.
The year we took office, Rhode Island saw just $84 million in commercial real estate investment. But by the end of our first term, we had record growth and over a billion dollars in new investment. The long-empty Route 195 land in Providence is now a hub of innovation, with two million square feet of new development underway, creating thousands of new jobs. We've also cut thousands of pages of unnecessary regulations and started the state's first small business loan program. And more than half of these loans have gone to minority- and women-owned businesses.
And I'm especially proud that we're building an economy that works for workers. In partnership with labor, we've increased wages for childcare and home care workers - jobs held primarily by women of color. We've guaranteed paid sick and family leave. Today, nearly every Rhode Islander has health insurance. We've codified Roe v. Wade. And we've raised the minimum wage four times. Let's keep going. Now, more than ever, hardworking people need a raise.
Over the last six years, we went from having the worst infrastructure in the country to more road construction than ever before, creating thousands of good, union jobs in the process. We've also grown our green economy, built the nation's first offshore wind farm, and put Rhode Island on a path to be the first state powered by 100% renewable energy by the end of this decade. Rhode Islanders can be proud that we're leading the way in the fight against climate change.
When it comes to our future, nothing is more important than our kids. Education is about equity. It's how we ensure every kid, in every neighborhood, gets a fair shot to realize his or her potential. Together, we've made record investments in K-12 education, and we've gone from crumbling school buildings to a billion-dollar, once-in-a-generation investment in school construction.
We quadrupled the number of public Pre-K classes and made all-day Kindergarten a reality for every child. We became the first state in America to teach computer science in every public school. We increased the number of high-quality career and technical training programs in our high schools by 60%, and we opened the Westerly Education Center. Later this year, we'll cut the ribbon on another education center in Woonsocket - replicating the successful model that has already provided skills for new jobs to over 3,000 students.
We took bold steps to make community college tuition-free for every high school graduate. At the time we did that, few states had taken this path. Now our country looks to us as a model. And since we started offering that scholarship to young people in Rhode Island, the two-year graduation rate at CCRI has tripled, and we've seen a 500% increase among students of color.
Last year, during this address, I stood here and warned that this program was set to expire, and if that happens, we would be pulling the rug out from under thousands of Rhode Islanders at the worst possible time. But tonight, I have good news. The Speaker and Senate President and a dozen of their colleagues have committed to removing that expiration and making this program permanent. So tonight, on behalf of thousands of CCRI students and graduates, and the countless more who will follow in their footsteps, I want to thank our legislative leaders for keeping this promise to Rhode Islanders and giving students a shot at the American Dream.
Rhode Island was founded on the principles of inclusion, acceptance and equality. We believe all people deserve to be treated with dignity and respect and should have the same opportunities for success - no matter your race, religion, gender, ethnicity, who you love or where you come from.
These principles have guided our determination to appoint judges who look like the people they serve. I'm proud to have appointed highly-qualified, talented judges at every level of our court system, including the most diverse group of judges in state history. And, for the first time ever, the Rhode Island Supreme Court is now multi-racial and majority-female.
For too long, our state's name was dragged down by a word so closely associated with the ugliest time in our history. We can't change our past, but we must acknowledge it and commit to a more inclusive future. Last year, the people of Rhode Island came together and made history, voting to finally remove the word "plantations" from the official name of our great state.
This is the Rhode Island we have built together over the last six years.
This is the foundation we have laid for a bright future - for a Rhode Island that brings everyone along. We have to keep up that fight.
Monday marked 11 months since the first case of COVID-19 in Rhode Island. For the past 11 months, we have traveled a difficult path, but we've held on to hope - just as our state flag inspires us to do.
I will always have hope in Rhode Island. This is the state that gave my grandfather hope when he first arrived from Italy as a teenager and taught himself English at the Providence Public Library. It's the state where for many years my dad, a Navy veteran and the first in his family to attend college, was able to support his family with a good job in manufacturing while he hoped for an even brighter life for his kids.
This is where my brother, sister and I grew up and rode the waves together at Sand Hill Cove Beach. It's where Andy and I were married, and Ceci and Tommy were born. It's where I started my business, and where I've had the honor to serve as your treasurer and as your governor. I'm forever grateful for the trust you placed in me these last ten years. Rhode Island is - and always will be - my home.
It is very difficult for me to leave Rhode Island. If I am confirmed as Commerce Secretary, it will be a privilege to serve in President Biden's cabinet as we rebuild America and lift up those who have been left behind - a continuation of the work we have done together these past six years.
Thank you, Rhode Island. Thank you for embracing me, embracing my family, and embracing change.
Thank you to my incredible team for your talent, your energy, your passion for public service, and your total commitment to building a stronger Rhode Island.
And thank you to Rhode Islanders in every community for believing in our vision. You have made me a better person and a better governor.
At my first inauguration, I promised you that I would wake up every day focused on expanding opportunities for families in Rhode Island no matter what obstacles were thrown in our path. I promised that I would go anywhere and work with anyone who wanted to do what was right for Rhode Island.
Although I will be working in Washington, the countless Rhode Islanders I have come to know will always be at the top of my mind. Every small business owner, every Promise student, everyone who had the courage to train for a new job, every woman breaking down barriers, every single Rhode Islander stepping up and giving back. You have my commitment that I will continue to wake up every day focused on making life better for you, and for all our fellow Americans.
Many years from now, we will look back at this year and everything we overcame together. I hope what we remember is the strength, determination and fortitude that carried us from some of the darkest days into a brighter, more prosperous, more equitable future.
I'd like to end tonight with a special message for girls and young women across Rhode Island. This world needs you. We need your voice. We need your ideas. We need you to lead.
When I was first asked to serve as Commerce Secretary, I was unsure. But it was the women in my life - my mother, my sister, and even my teenage daughter - who gave me the push I needed. They told me it was okay to be nervous, but that I had to look within myself and summon the courage to lead.
So, to all the young women out there, I want to leave you with their words. Look within yourself, and summon the courage to lead. There will be plenty of times when you're unsure. In those moments, we need you to push aside your doubt and fear, and to say yes. Know that you can be anything you want to be. You are strong and smart and capable.
And I'm looking forward to the day when one of you is our governor.
Thank you. God bless you. And God bless Rhode Island.
-Gina Raimondo Governor
---
| | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903
---
| [Unsubscribe dpwillis67@gmail.com](https://visitor.constantcontact.com/do?p=un&m=001_rvij1smacIHQplL2vH8YA%3D&ch=e9e38b2e-7e90-11ea-b887-d4ae5292c4bc&ca=2a1de25c-0cd7-4310-a2bd-81e9f3fafb54)
---

Sent by [communications@governor.ri.gov](mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov) powered by

These principles have guided our determination to appoint judges who look like the people they serve. I'm proud to have appointed highly-qualified, talented judges at every level of our court system, including the most diverse group of judges in state history. And, for the first time ever, the Rhode Island Supreme Court is now multi-racial and majority-female. For too long, our state's name was dragged down by a word so closely associated with the ugliest time in our history. We can't change our past, but we must acknowledge it and commit to a more inclusive future. Last year, the people of Rhode Island came together and made history, voting to finally remove the word "plantations" from the official name of our great state. This is the Rhode Island we have built together over the last six years. This is the foundation we have laid for a bright future - for a Rhode Island that brings everyone along. We have to keep up that fight. Monday marked 11 months since the first case of COVID-19 in Rhode Island. For the past 11 months, we have traveled a difficult path, but we've held on to hope - just as our state flag inspires us to do. I will always have hope in Rhode Island. This is the state that gave my grandfather hope when he first arrived from Italy as a teenager and taught himself English at the Providence Public Library. It's the state where for many years my dad, a Navy veteran and the first in his family to attend college, was able to support his family with a good job in manufacturing while he hoped for an even brighter life for his kids. This is where my brother, sister and I grew up and rode the waves together at Sand Hill Cove Beach. It's where Andy and I were married, and Ceci and Tommy were born. It's where I started my business, and where I've had the honor to serve as your treasurer and as your governor. I'm forever grateful for the trust you placed in me these last ten years. Rhode Island is - and always will be - my home. It is very difficult for me to leave Rhode Island. If I am confirmed as Commerce Secretary, it will be a privilege to serve in President Biden's cabinet as we rebuild America and lift up those who have been left behind - a continuation of the work we have done together these past six years. Thank you, Rhode Island. Thank you for embracing me, embracing my family, and embracing change. Thank you to my incredible team for your talent, your energy, your passion for public service, and your total commitment to building a stronger Rhode Island. And thank you to Rhode Islanders in every community for believing in our vision. You have made me a better person and a better governor. At my first inauguration, I promised you that I would wake up every day focused on expanding opportunities for families in Rhode Island no matter what obstacles were thrown in our path. I promised that I would go anywhere and work with anyone who wanted to do what was right for Rhode Island. Although I will be working in Washington, the countless Rhode Islanders I have come to know will always be at the top of my mind. Every small business owner, every Promise student, everyone who had the courage to train for a new job, every woman breaking down barriers, every single Rhode Islander stepping up and giving back. You have my commitment that I will continue to wake up every day focused on making life better for you, and for all our fellow Americans. Many years from now, we will look back at this year and everything we overcame together. I hope what we remember is the strength, determination and fortitude that carried us from some of the darkest days into a brighter, more prosperous, more equitable future. I'd like to end tonight with a special message for girls and young women across Rhode Island. This world needs you. We need your voice. We need your ideas. We need you to lead. When I was first asked to serve as Commerce Secretary, I was unsure. But it was the women in my life - my mother, my sister, and even my teenage daughter - who gave me the push I needed. They told me it was okay to be nervous, but that I had to look within myself and summon the courage to lead. So, to all the young women out there, I want to leave you with their words. Look within yourself, and summon the courage to lead. There will be plenty of times when you're unsure. In those moments, we need you to push aside your doubt and fear, and to say yes. Know that you can be anything you want to be. You are strong and smart and capable. And I'm looking forward to the day when one of you is our governor. Thank you. God bless you. And God bless Rhode Island. -Gina Raimondo Governor | | Governor's Office | 82 Smith Street, Providence, RI 02903 | | Sent by mailto:communications@governor.ri.gov powered by

name: Becca Ellison, Evergreen Action
subject: 🛑 Stop Investing in Pollution [NEW MEMO]
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION
body:
Momentum is on our side.
| [](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/379?t=3&akid=6448%2E53494%2EwTBSs0) Friend, Fossil fuel subsidies are making headlines again, but this time, we're making progress. Fossil fuel pollution is accelerating climate change and giving people asthma, lung cancer, heart
disease, and other life-threatening health problems. And yet the federal government gives more than $20 billion in direct financial support to fossil fuel companies each year. And if that weren't enough, a 2019 International Monetary Fund report found that when we account for the costs of pollution, the U.S. government pays well over _$600 billion annually_ to fossil fuel corporations—a staggering sum of taxpayer dollars that these companies did not earn. America must stop investing in pollution—and it can. **[Read our new policy memo to learn how the U.S. government can stop investing in pollution so we can start investing in our clean energy future.](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/2016?t=4&akid=6448%2E53494%2EwTBSs0)** | [LEARN MORE ](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/2016?t=5&akid=6448%2E53494%2EwTBSs0)
---
Momentum is on our side: Just last week, President Biden issued executive orders temporarily stopping new oil and gas permits on public lands and promising to "identify and end" all fossil fuel subsidies.
Our new memo goes into detail on _how_ the federal government can eliminate direct financial support for the fossil fuel industry, end corporate exploitation of our public lands and waters, and ensure that corporations shoulder the full cost of the damage they cause—because the public shouldn't have to foot the bill for climate damage while fossil fuel CEOs line their pockets.
**[Download our new policy memo to learn how the Biden administration and Congress can stop propping up the fossil fuel industry with taxpayer dollars.](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/2016?t=6&akid=6448%2E53494%2EwTBSs0)**
The first step in getting out of a hole is to stop digging. By stopping the flow of subsidies and special breaks to Big Polluters, we can stop the climate crisis, improve public health, and build a more just and equitable economy.
Thank you,
Becca Ellison
Policy Manager, Evergreen Action
P.S. Short on time? **[Watch and share our video on Facebook to learn more about fossil fuel subsidies and how we can end them.](https://act.evergreenaction.com/go/2018?t=7&akid=6448%2E53494%2EwTBSs0)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. [Click here to unsubscribe](https://act.evergreenaction.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=8&akid=6448%2E53494%2EwTBSs0).Contributions or gifts to EVERGREEN ACTION are not tax deductible.| PAID FOR BY EVERGREEN ACTION
---
All content © 2021 EVERGREEN ACTION, All Rights Reserved

name: Laura Loomer
subject: Just got off the air
body:
I just did an appearance on InfoWars with Alex Jones to talk about my Red Flagging by the FBI.<
| | | | Friend,

[](https://em.loomered.com/ga/click/2-587068045-4281-412102-968524-5420393-e460d8c457-0444dc6eed) I just did an appearance on InfoWars with Alex Jones to talk about my Red Flagging by the FBI.

**What happened to me is going to happen to many more American Conservatives if this isn't halted immediately.**

When an American goes to purchase a firearm, their information is run through the FBI’s NICS database.

If your name ends up on one of the 12 categories for rejection, then you’re denied.

Not only are you denied, but **you’re immediately stripped of your right to even POSSESS a firearm** if your name is in this database.

One of those 12 reasons for denial I call the “open category”, which the FBI calls the “Federally Denied Persons File” – AKA, “**Any Reason They Want File** ”, comes with an asterisk that says:

**“The Federally Denied Persons File is compiled of individuals that are predetermined to have firearm prohibitions without listing the specific category.”**
I’m confident that I was not denied under any of the other 11 reasons, so that leaves the “open category” as a possible reason for denial because the FBI won’t say why they stripped me of my Second Amendment rights. And I was never notified about being on this list, even when I had a firearm.

[**Help me fight this today.**](https://em.loomered.com/ga/click/2-587068045-4281-412102-968524-5420393-e460d8c457-0444dc6eed)

I did some research and in all of 2020, only 24 Americans were denied a firearm purchase because they’re on that “Federally Denied Persons File.”

I suspect that number will expand exponentially during the Biden Administration and I’m leading the fight to not only get my rights back, but to show others how to do the same when it happens to them.

So far, we’ve raised nearly $8,000 to hire the attorney and cover the costs of challenging my denial . . . **but we have another $12,000 to raise**.

[**Will you donate now?**](https://em.loomered.com/ga/click/2-587068045-4281-412102-968524-5420393-e460d8c457-0444dc6eed)

Even $10 can help us get closer to our goal. [**Click here to donate securely online.**](https://em.loomered.com/ga/click/2-587068045-4281-412102-968524-5420393-e460d8c457-0444dc6eed)

Thank you so much for all that you do and I’ll be in touch very soon with updates.

Respectfully,

Laura Loomer

---
(C) 2021 Illoominate Media
720 Lucerne Ave, #1465
LAKE WORTH, FL. 33460
[Unsubscribe](https://em.loomered.com/ga/unsubscribe/2-587068045-4281-412102-968524-7b079168d98543a-0444dc6eed)

---
